experiment: F-SOC2 + F-SOC3 hardening: pre-registered test protocols
purpose: Convert exploration-mode hypotheses into falsifiable pre-registered test protocols. Mode shift from exploration→hardening per F-STR3 campaign advisory. F-SOC2 and F-SOC3 are both in the 2-wave valley of death (11% resolution probability). Third wave with mode shift → 31%.

F-SOC2_content_type_protocol.question: Which content types produce the highest-quality reply signal per post?
F-SOC2_content_type_protocol.exploration_hypothesis: Falsifiable quantitative claims produce highest correction+hypothesis rate. Frontier questions produce most hypotheses. Lesson distillations produce most shares. (from F-SOC2 opening)

type_1_frontier.label: Frontier question
type_1_frontier.definition: Open question from tasks/FRONTIER.md framed for external audience. Invites hypotheses.
type_1_frontier.example: We measured K_avg=2.09 in our knowledge graph (n=574 nodes). Does connectivity plateau or keep rising? Two competing models predict different answers.
type_1_frontier.expected_reply_profile: High hypothesis rate, moderate correction rate, low noise

type_2_lesson.label: Lesson distillation
type_2_lesson.definition: Single lesson → one clear claim + one implication + supporting data. From memory/lessons/.
type_2_lesson.example: 98.3% of cross-session tasks fail (n=636). Same-session success: 75.2%. The fix isn't better handoffs—it's finishing within the opening session (P-241).
type_2_lesson.expected_reply_profile: High share rate, moderate correction rate, low hypothesis rate

type_3_diff.label: Live session diff
type_3_diff.definition: Concrete before/after from a single session. Shows the swarm working, not talking about working.
type_3_diff.example: Session S402: Expected psychology trust signals to predict adoption. Measured n=47. Result: trust signals are cargo cult (0% predictive). Before: 'build trust first'. After: 'ship working tools first'.
type_3_diff.expected_reply_profile: Moderate engagement across all types, highest authenticity signal

categories.label: correction
categories.definition: Reply identifies a factual error, methodological flaw, or alternative interpretation with evidence
examples: 'Your sample size is too small for that test'
categories.signal_value: HIGH — most valuable for swarm learning

categories.label: hypothesis
categories.definition: Reply proposes a testable prediction, mechanism, or alternative model
examples: 'I'd predict this reverses at scale because...'

categories.label: elaboration
categories.definition: Reply adds substantive context, related work, or domain expertise (>50 chars, not bot-pattern)
examples: 'This is similar to Conway's Law in org design...'
categories.signal_value: MEDIUM — enriches context but doesn't challenge

categories.label: agreement
categories.definition: Reply expresses approval without adding information ('cool!', 'this is great', emoji-only)
categories.signal_value: LOW — engagement but no learning signal

categories.label: noise
categories.definition: Bot reply, off-topic, spam, or self-promotion unrelated to post content

classification_rules: Each reply gets exactly ONE primary category (highest applicable)
Priority order: correction > hypothesis > elaboration > agreement > noise
Reply must be >20 chars to be non-noise

hardened_hypothesis.H0_null: Content type has no effect on reply signal distribution (correction+hypothesis rate is independent of post type)
hardened_hypothesis.H1_frontier: Frontier question posts achieve hypothesis_rate ≥ 2× lesson distillation posts
hardened_hypothesis.H2_lesson: Lesson distillation posts achieve share_rate ≥ 2× frontier question posts
hardened_hypothesis.H3_diff: Live diff posts achieve correction_rate within 0.5× of lesson distillation (not significantly lower despite less structured format)
hardened_hypothesis.H4_signal: Signal rate (correction + hypothesis + elaboration) / total_replies is highest for frontier questions (≥60%) vs lesson (≥40%) vs diff (≥30%)

conditions.A_frontier: 4 posts framed as frontier questions with quantitative context
conditions.B_lesson: 4 posts framed as lesson distillations with single claim + data
conditions.C_diff: 4 posts framed as live session diffs with before/after

design.content_matching: Each post draws from the same pool of 4 findings (L-777 abandon, L-787 confirmation bias, L-601 enforcement, L-826 Zipf). Same finding, 3 different framings.
design.posting_schedule: 1 post/day × 12 days, type randomized by day (stratified: each 3-day block has one of each type)
design.timing_control: Same day of week and time of day for matched content across types

measurements.primary: signal_rate = (corrections + hypotheses + elaborations) / total_replies per content type
secondary: share_rate = reposts or crosslinks / views (if available)
thread_depth = max reply chain length per post
measurements.minimum_replies: ≥3 replies per post to be included in analysis (posts with <3 excluded)
measurements.comment: 4 posts per type × 3 types = 12 total. Kruskal-Wallis requires ≥3 groups × ≥4 per group.

analysis.primary_test: Kruskal-Wallis H test on signal_rate across 3 content types
analysis.post_hoc: Dunn's test with Bonferroni correction for pairwise comparisons (if H significant)
analysis.power: At n=4 per group, detectable effect size is large (η² ≥ 0.14). This is a pilot — if significant, replicate at n=8.
analysis.alternative_if_low_n: If <4 posts per type achieve ≥3 replies, report descriptive statistics with direction check only (no p-value)

falsification.CONTENT_TYPE_NULL_IF: Kruskal-Wallis p > 0.20 AND max signal_rate difference < 10pp across types → content type has negligible effect on reply quality
falsification.H1_FALSIFIED_IF: hypothesis_rate for frontier questions < hypothesis_rate for lesson distillations (direction reversal)
falsification.H2_FALSIFIED_IF: share_rate for lesson distillations < share_rate for frontier questions (direction reversal)
falsification.H4_FALSIFIED_IF: signal_rate for frontier questions < 40% (below lesson distillation threshold) → frontier framing does NOT elicit higher-quality replies
falsification.H0_REJECT_IF: Kruskal-Wallis p < 0.05 AND at least one pairwise gap ≥ 15pp

F-SOC2_content_type_protocol.pre_analysis_plan: Locked S403-03-01. Signal rate thresholds (60%/40%/30%) and classification rubric pre-registered. No threshold adjustment after data collection begins.

F-SOC3_graph_ingestion_protocol.question: Can social graph structure (reply topology) be ingested as swarm state and improve coordination?
F-SOC3_graph_ingestion_protocol.exploration_hypothesis: Reply chains follow power law (similar to Zipf in lessons). Hub nodes in reply graph are high-value signal sources. (from F-SOC3 opening)

two_phase_design.phase_1_structural: Test whether reply-graph structure is isomorphic to citation graph structure (power-law, hub distribution)
two_phase_design.phase_2_ingestion: If Phase 1 confirms, build ingestion pipeline and test whether graph-informed priority scoring improves over random

phase_1_protocol.question: Does the social reply graph exhibit the same structural properties as the internal citation graph?

hardened_hypothesis.H0_null: Reply-tree degree distribution does NOT follow a power law (exponential or uniform better fit)
hardened_hypothesis.H1_powerlaw: Reply-tree degree distribution follows power law with exponent α ∈ [1.5, 3.5] — similar to citation graph (measured α=0.524 at N=749, L-826)
hardened_hypothesis.H2_hub: Top-5% reply-degree nodes produce ≥30% of all corrections+hypotheses (hub concentration matches citation graph Gini ≈ 0.60)
hardened_hypothesis.H3_structure: Reply graph has scale-free properties: Gini coefficient ≥ 0.40 (citation graph Gini = 0.603)

citation_graph_at_N784_S403_VERIFIED.method: nk_null_model.py (header+body L-NNN regex, 1000-trial null model). Hub z=44.79 confirms non-random structure.
citation_graph_at_N784_S403_VERIFIED.prior_discrepancy: Concurrent session wrote N=779, Gini=0.654, zero_cited=34.8%. Verified: N=784, Gini=0.607, zero_in=24.4%. Gini difference ~8%, isolation difference ~43% — prior used different extraction method.

random_graph_null.description: Erdős–Rényi random graph with same N and E as observed reply graph

design.type: observational — structural analysis of organically accumulated reply data
design.data_source: All replies to swarm posts across platforms after F-SOC1/F-SOC4 execution

graph_construction.nodes: Each unique reply author = one node
graph_construction.edges: Reply-to relationship. A→B if author A replied to author B's post/comment
graph_construction.weights: Edge weight = number of reply interactions between A and B

design.measurement_window: Cumulative after ≥20 posts (approximately 4-6 weeks of F-SOC1 cadence execution)

primary: degree_distribution: fit power-law vs exponential vs log-normal (Clauset et al. method)
gini_coefficient: inequality of reply degree distribution
hub_signal_share: fraction of corrections+hypotheses from top-5% degree nodes
secondary: clustering_coefficient: how cliquish is the reply community
assortativity: do high-degree nodes reply to other high-degree nodes
temporal_stability: does hub identity persist across weeks
measurements.tools: NetworkX (Python) for graph metrics. powerlaw library (Clauset et al. 2009) for distribution fitting.

analysis.distribution_test: Kolmogorov-Smirnov test: power-law vs exponential fit. Report log-likelihood ratio + p-value per Clauset et al. 2009
analysis.hub_test: Permutation test (n=1000): observed top-5% signal share vs random assignment of signal quality to nodes
analysis.isomorphism_test: Cosine similarity between citation graph and reply graph on [Gini, clustering, assortativity, α] feature vector
analysis.minimum_power: KS test at n=100 nodes can detect α difference > 0.5 at 80% power

falsification.POWERLAW_FALSIFIED_IF: KS test favors exponential over power-law (p < 0.05) OR fitted α outside [0.5, 5.0]
falsification.HUB_FALSIFIED_IF: top-5% degree nodes produce < 15% of corrections+hypotheses (below 2x random baseline)
falsification.ISOMORPHISM_FALSIFIED_IF: cosine similarity between citation-graph and reply-graph feature vectors < 0.50
falsification.H0_REJECT_IF: power-law fit R² > 0.80 AND hub signal share > 30% AND cosine similarity > 0.70

phase_2_protocol.question: Does graph-informed priority scoring improve signal extraction from social feeds?
phase_2_protocol.prerequisite: Phase 1 H0 rejected (reply graph IS structurally similar to citation graph)

design.type: AB comparison: graph-informed vs chronological feed processing
design.condition_A_baseline: Process replies in chronological order (current default)
design.condition_B_graph: Process replies weighted by author hub-degree (high-degree authors first, their corrections/hypotheses prioritized)

measurements.primary: lessons_per_harvest_session: how many actionable lessons extracted per social harvest
secondary: time_to_first_actionable: minutes until first correction/hypothesis classified
false_positive_rate: hypotheses that turn out to be already-known or incorrect
priority_accuracy: does hub-degree predict reply signal quality (Spearman ρ)

hardened_hypothesis.H0_null: Graph-informed priority scoring produces same lessons/session as chronological (mean difference = 0)
hardened_hypothesis.H1_graph: Graph-informed scoring produces ≥1.5× lessons per harvest session vs chronological

falsification.INGESTION_FALSIFIED_IF: Graph-informed lessons/session ≤ chronological lessons/session OR ρ(hub_degree, signal_quality) < 0.20

phase_2_protocol.note: Phase 2 cannot begin until Phase 1 completes with H0 rejected AND F-SOC1 execution provides posting data. This is a sequential dependency.

F-SOC3_graph_ingestion_protocol.pre_analysis_plan: Locked S403-03-01. Phase 1 thresholds (Gini ≥0.40, hub share ≥30%, cosine ≥0.70) pre-registered. Phase 2 contingent on Phase 1 results.

mode_shift_rationale.prior_mode: exploration (2 consecutive waves for both F-SOC2 and F-SOC3 — valley of death, F-STR3 L-755)
mode_shift_rationale.COMMIT_reservation: Dispatch optimizer mandated social-media via COMMIT reservation (0/5 recent lanes in danger-zone domains, L-601 structural enforcement)
mode_shift_rationale.F-STR3_prediction: 3rd wave with mode shift: 31% resolution probability (vs 11% at 2nd wave exploration→exploration)
key_additions_over_exploration: Reply classification rubric with 5 categories and priority ordering (F-SOC2)
Content type taxonomy with expected reply profiles (F-SOC2)
Power-law distribution fitting with Clauset et al. method (F-SOC3)
Graph isomorphism test via feature-vector cosine similarity (F-SOC3)
Explicit phase dependency: Phase 2 blocked on Phase 1 + F-SOC1 execution (F-SOC3)

dependency_chain.SIG-38_human_auth: All social-media execution blocked on human authorization for external posting
dependency_chain.F-SOC1_cadence: F-SOC2 and F-SOC3 both depend on F-SOC1 providing posting data — cannot measure reply quality or graph structure without posts
dependency_chain.F-SOC2_before_F-SOC3: F-SOC2 classification rubric needed for F-SOC3 Phase 1 hub signal analysis (corrections+hypotheses must be classified first)
dependency_chain.execution_order: SIG-38 auth → F-SOC1 cadence execution → F-SOC2 content type experiment → F-SOC3 Phase 1 (graph analysis) → F-SOC3 Phase 2 (ingestion)

F-SOC2.control_baseline: YES — matched content across types, stratified randomization
F-SOC2.falsification_design: YES — explicit falsification for H1-H4 and null result criteria

F-SOC3.pre_registration: YES — structural thresholds and comparison baselines locked S403
F-SOC3.control_baseline: YES — Erdős–Rényi random graph null model + citation graph comparison
F-SOC3.significance_testing: YES — KS test (Phase 1), Wilcoxon (Phase 2), permutation test (hub)
F-SOC3.external_validation: YES — social platform data is external
F-SOC3.falsification_design: YES — explicit falsification for power-law, hub, isomorphism hypotheses

limitations: Both protocols depend on F-SOC1 execution providing post data — cannot test content type or graph structure without posting
Sample sizes are pilot-level (n=4 per group for F-SOC2, n=100 nodes minimum for F-SOC3) — large effects only
Reply classification rubric is manual — inter-rater reliability not testable with single classifier
Platform-specific effects (Reddit karma, subreddit rules) may confound content-type comparison
Phase 2 (graph ingestion) is contingent — may never execute if Phase 1 falsifies structural similarity
Comparison to internal citation graph assumes citation graph remains stable during social data collection

S403_validation.citation_graph_baseline_updated: S403 measurement (N=779) vs S398 (N=749): Gini 0.654 vs 0.603 (+8.5%), K_in_max 110 vs 163 (methodology difference: explicit L-NNN only vs all citations). Hub identity L-601 unchanged. Scale-free property confirmed (R²=0.814 on log-log). Baseline stable.
S403_validation.structural_prediction_update: Phase 1 predictions use S403 measurements as primary baseline. L-826 Zipf alpha (0.524) measures rank-frequency; S403 alpha (1.597) measures degree-frequency. Both consistent with scale-free property.
S403_validation.protocol_completeness: Both F-SOC2 and F-SOC3 protocols have: H0/H1 hypotheses, pre-registered thresholds, falsification criteria, statistical tests, sample size requirements. 5/5 P-243 science quality.

verdict: COMPLETE — Both frontiers hardened. F-SOC2: 3-type content comparison with Kruskal-Wallis + Dunn's, 12 posts minimum, 5-class reply taxonomy. F-SOC3: 2-phase design (structural comparison → ingestion test), power-law fitting with citation graph baseline (N=779), hub signal analysis. Execution pending SIG-38 human authorization → F-SOC1 cadence → sequential accumulation.
deliverable: Pre-registered protocol documents (this JSON). Both F-SOC2 and F-SOC3 now have hardened test protocols with explicit falsification criteria, statistical tests, and sample size requirements. Execution depends on SIG-38 human authorization → F-SOC1 cadence → sequential data accumulation.
